Worker.instruction: sets local source variable which points to the consumer that it is connected to. will include the host and port information for the source

methods.name: AddPrompt
methods.description: Admits a user prompt into the continuous-batching engine. Tokenizes the lowercased a-z content; on a chat cache-hit it reuses the cached prefix/KV state (continue_with), otherwise it starts a fresh sequence (loading prior chat history from the chat DB). Returns accepted + seq_id, or accepted=false with reason 'worker full' (batch at max_active) or 'empty prompt'. On accept it announces {run_id} on the redis runs:started stream and streams typed token entries to tokens:<message_id>. Mid-run: a prompt added while a batch is running joins at the next ~0.25s step - it never blocks the in-flight step, never waits for the current batch to drain, and is never dropped (the only non-join outcome is the 'worker full' reject). The worker steps a snapshot of the active set taken at the start of each tick and does all inference outside the lock, so add_prompt holds the lock only for an O(1) append; calling AddPrompt while the worker is mid-inference (partway through a token's layer passes) is safe and the new sequence is picked up on the following tick.

Returns accepted + seq_id, or accepted=false with reason 'worker full' (batch at max_active) or 'empty prompt'. On accept it announces {run_id} on the redis runs:started stream and streams typed token entries to tokens:<message_id>. Mid-run: a prompt added while a batch is running joins at the next ~0.25s step - it never blocks the in-flight step, never waits for the current batch to drain, and is never dropped (the only non-join outcome is the 'worker full' reject). The worker steps a snapshot of the active set taken at the start of each tick and does all inference outside the lock, so add_prompt holds the lock only for an O(1) append; calling AddPrompt while the worker is mid-inference (partway through a token's layer passes) is safe and the new sequence is picked up on the following tick.

methods.name: GetStatus
methods.description: Reports the worker's live batch capacity: has_space (room for another prompt), active_count (sequences currently decoding), cached_count (idle prefix-cached sequences), and max_active (the configured cap, read live from worker.json). Callers/schedulers use it to decide whether to route a prompt to this worker.

history.change: Reports the worker's live batch capacity: has_space (room for another prompt), active_count (sequences currently decoding), cached_count (idle prefix-cached sequences), and max_active (the configured cap, read live from worker.json). Callers/schedulers use it to decide whether to route a prompt to this worker.

methods.name: UpdateConsumer
methods.description: Records the paired feeding consumer's host:port on the engine (engine.source) so the worker can call back into it - e.g. the on_cache_evict hook invoking Consumer.OnChatEvict when a chat's prefix cache is evicted. Returns ok.

history.change: Records the paired feeding consumer's host:port on the engine (engine.source) so the worker can call back into it - e.g. the on_cache_evict hook invoking Consumer.OnChatEvict when a chat's prefix cache is evicted. Returns ok.

Consumer.instruction: updates the workers local variable with new workers this consumer connects to. will be given host and port for each worker

methods.name: OnChatEvict
methods.description: remove the chat_id from the local chat_id to worker map
